stories.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
stories.simple_summary: The U.S. military has sent over 100 robot vehicles without drivers to fight in Ukraine. These are remote-controlled or self-driving trucks and similar vehicles.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect you directly — it's about military technology in a war zone.

stories.title: 3 Millionaire-Maker Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks
stories.simple_summary: An investment newsletter is recommending three AI-related companies as stocks they think could make people rich.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on unless you're actively buying stocks.

stories.title: Exponential Growth Forecast in AI Cognitive Computing Market: From $45.5 Billion in 2025 to $157.26 Billion by 2030
stories.simple_summary: Market analysts predict that AI computing technology businesses will make a lot more money in the coming years (growing from about $45 billion to $157 billion).
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and large tech companies — it doesn't change how AI affects your daily life.

stories.title: Explosive Growth: AI-Powered Debt Risk Platform Market to Surge from $6.61 Billion in 2025 to $23.77 Billion by 2030
stories.simple_summary: Financial experts predict that AI software designed to spot risky loans will become a much bigger business over the next five years.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and banks — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar
stories.simple_summary: Samsung, a big electronics company, made much more profit recently because so many companies want to buy the special computer chips that power AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI is booming in business, but it doesn't directly change what you'd pay for a Samsung phone or TV.

stories.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
stories.simple_summary: Last week's news said an AI program pulled off a hacking attack all by itself, but it turns out a human actually chose the victim and did most of the setup work — the AI only did the final technical steps.
stories.why_it_matters: This is good news for cybersecurity — AI isn't taking over hacking the way the scary headlines suggested. Humans are still running the show.

stories.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
stories.simple_summary: SK Hynix, a company that makes computer memory chips for AI, is getting so much business that it's planning to sell stock to American investors for several billion dollars.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mostly to investors — nothing you need to do, but it shows how much demand there is for AI technology.

stories.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: What to watch for in the second half
stories.simple_summary: A business news newsletter is previewing stories about China and economics in the coming months.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a preview of upcoming news — not a story itself.

stories.title: Gov. JB Pritzker signs Illinois AI regulations into law, aiming to rein in 'the tech bros'
stories.simple_summary: Illinois's governor signed a new law to make rules about how AI companies can use people's information and make decisions. The law is meant to stop big tech companies from doing whatever they want.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live or do business in Illinois, this gives you some protection — companies now have to be more careful with your data and explain how their AI makes important decisions.

stories.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
stories.simple_summary: A tech company leader is discussing whether AI systems should keep 'thinking models' and 'action agents' separate from each other, or combine them.
stories.why_it_matters: This is technical talk for engineers and investors — nothing that affects you directly.

stories.title: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signs AI bill into law
stories.simple_summary: Illinois's governor signed a new law to regulate how AI companies operate in the state.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live or do business in Illinois, this means companies have to follow new rules about how they use AI with your information and make automatic decisions.

stories.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
stories.simple_summary: Apple is updating Siri (its voice assistant) so you can change how fast it talks and how expressive it sounds. Apple is also rebuilding Siri with new AI technology.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use an iPhone or iPad, Siri will soon sound more natural and personal to you, and you'll have more control over how it sounds.
